displayTitle: AC Hugs the Surface, DC Goes Deep

beats.narration: Where does the field come from, and what current drives it? Those two choices set how deep you see and how particles move.

payload.title: SOURCE & CURRENT
payload.sub: Two choices that set depth and particle mobility.

beats.narration: Three sources: the permanent magnet — no power, no sparks; the electromagnetic yoke, proven by its lifting test; and the bench unit for production.

payload.heading: Three ways to make the field

beats.narration: Alternating current reverses constantly, pinning its field to the surface — the skin effect. Half-wave DC pulses and penetrates. Full-wave DC drives the field deepest.

payload.heading: Current type sets field depth
payload.note: AC SKIN · HWDC PULSES · FWDC DEEPEST

beats.narration: Choose on purpose. Surface cracks: AC, with lively particles. Rough welds, dry powder, slight depth: half-wave DC. Deepest bench shots: full-wave DC.
The rule: AC hugs the surface, DC goes deep. Choose the current on purpose — then prove the field, like always.

payload.sub: Choose the current on purpose — then prove the field.
